title: Docker references with both a tag and digest are currently not supported
body:
Skopeo is unable to copy images with both a tag and digest....

```sh
skopeo copy docker://alpine:3.12.1@sha256:185518070891758909c9f839cf4ca393ee977ac378609f700f60a771a2dfe321 dir:/tmp/
FATA[0000] Invalid source name docker://alpine:3.12.1@sha256:185518070891758909c9f839cf4ca393ee977ac378609f700f60a771a2dfe321: Docker references with both a tag and digest are currently not supported
```

...but podman has no problem:

```sh
podman pull alpine:3.12.1@sha256:185518070891758909c9f839cf4ca393ee977ac378609f700f60a771a2dfe321
Resolved "alpine" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine@sha256:185518070891758909c9f839cf4ca393ee977ac378609f700f60a771a2dfe321...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob df20fa9351a1 done   | 
Copying config a24bb40132 done   | 
Writing manifest to image destination
a24bb4013296f61e89ba57005a7b3e52274d8edd3ae2077d04395f806b63d83e
```

This was brought up before in #2367 and dismissed as a library problem, but it looks like this has been fixed in the lib for two years in containers/common#1505.  Can skopeo support this as podman does?

comments.body:
Actually, perhaps I'm misled by podman.  If I change the above tag and keep the SHA, it still pulls based on SHA.

```sh
podman pull alpine:3.14.1@sha256:185518070891758909c9f839cf4ca393ee977ac378609f700f60a771a2dfe321
Resolved "alpine" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine@sha256:185518070891758909c9f839cf4ca393ee977ac378609f700f60a771a2dfe321...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob df20fa9351a1 done   | 
Copying config a24bb40132 done   | 
Writing manifest to image destination
a24bb4013296f61e89ba57005a7b3e52274d8edd3ae2077d04395f806b63d83e
```

Nevertheless, my question remains, since it looks like containers/common#1505 should address this issue.

Thanks for reaching out.

> Actually, perhaps I'm misled by podman. If I change the above tag and keep the SHA, it still pulls based on SHA.

Yes, exactly, _and that what the vocal users want_ (they don’t want the systems to go down when a tag is moved, but they want the tag to exist “as a comment”). I don’t think such a confusing/misleading feature makes sense to provide in a low-level tool like Skopeo. (And, as mentioned in #2367, Skopeo is mostly a thin wrapper over c/image.)

Podman does that for Docker compatibility via the c/common code, that’s on top of c/image. Skopeo is lower-level and does not use any of that.
